projectContext.stack: React + Vite, Supabase, Tailwind CSS, React Router v6

design.tailwindTokens: dark, paper, rust are already extended in tailwind.config.js

supabaseTables.friendships: id, user_id, friend_id, status (pending/accepted), tier (friend/fof), created_at
supabaseTables.quests: id, title, description, duration_min, xp, context_tags[], created_at
supabaseTables.quest_sessions: id, quest_id, user_id, party_ids[], started_at, completed_at, photo_url, elapsed_sec, xp_earned

keyFiles.src/screens/Login.jsx: magic link form, rust logo, dark bg
keyFiles.src/screens/AuthCallback.jsx: checks users table, routes to /onboarding or /home
keyFiles.supabase/functions/drop-quest/index.ts: picks random quest, writes to active_quest

prompts.7:
Configure the Side/Quest React app as an installable Progressive Web App (PWA).
Current state: React + Vite app deployed to Vercel.
Build:
1. Install vite-plugin-pwa: npm install vite-plugin-pwa --save-dev
2. Update vite.config.js to add the VitePWA plugin with:
   - name: "Side / Quest"
   - short_name: "SideQuest"
   - theme_color: "#1a1612"
   - background_color: "#1a1612"
   - display: "standalone"
   - icons: generate a simple SVG icon (a circle with "S/Q" text) and reference it as 192x192 and 512x512
   - registerType: 'autoUpdate'
3. Add a manifest.json to the public/ folder manually as a fallback
4. In the Home screen, add a small "Install app" banner at the bottom that:
   - Listens for the "beforeinstallprompt" event
   - Shows when the event fires (Chrome/Android)
   - Shows iOS instructions ("Tap share → Add to Home Screen") when on Safari iOS (detect via navigator.userAgent)
   - Dismisses when tapped
5. Output the full updated vite.config.js and any new files
Note: On iOS Safari, the user must manually add to home screen. Add a comment explaining this.
prompts.10:
Build a React hook that listens for quest drops in real time using Supabase Realtime.
Context:
- Supabase client at src/lib/supabase.js
- The "active_quest" table gets a new row inserted when a quest drops (from the drop-quest Edge Function)
- We want all connected clients to know immediately
Build:
1. A hook: src/hooks/useQuestDrop.js that:
   - Subscribes to INSERT events on the "active_quest" table using supabase.channel()
   - Returns { activeQuest, loading } where activeQuest is the current active quest joined with its quest details
   - On mount, also fetches the current active_quest row (in case the app opened mid-quest)
   - Cleans up subscription on unmount
2. Enable Realtime on the active_quest table:
   - Output the SQL to run in Supabase: ALTER TABLE active_quest REPLICA IDENTITY FULL;
   - And the Supabase dashboard step: Database → Replication → enable active_quest
3. In the Home screen, use this hook:
   - If no active quest: show "No quest right now" + a "Simulate drop" button (calls the Edge Function for testing)
   - If active quest: show a banner "▲ QUEST IS LIVE" with the quest title and a button "See quest →" that navigates to /quest
Keep the Realtime subscription efficient — unsubscribe on component unmount.
prompts.11:
Write the Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies for the Side/Quest app.
Tables to secure: users, friendships, quest_sessions, reactions, presence, active_quest, quests
Rules:
- users: anyone can read (for friend profiles), only the owner can update their own row
- friendships: users can read friendships where they are user_id or friend_id; can insert where user_id = auth.uid()
- quest_sessions: users can read their own sessions and sessions where their id is in party_ids; can insert/update their own
- reactions: anyone in a session's party can read; authenticated users can insert their own
- presence: users can read all presence (needed for proximity); can only insert/update their own
- active_quest: anyone authenticated can read; only service role can write (Edge Function handles writes)
- quests: anyone authenticated can read; no user writes
Output:
1. Enable RLS on all tables (ALTER TABLE x ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY)
2. All CREATE POLICY statements
3. A note: "Run this entire script in the Supabase SQL Editor after the schema migration from Task 1"
Write output to: supabase/rls-policies.sql
prompts.13:
Build the location presence system for Side/Quest.
Context:
- There is a "presence" table: user_id (PK), lat, lng, updated_at
- We write location only when a quest is active, and delete it after
- Supabase client at src/lib/supabase.js
Build:
1. src/lib/location.js — two exported async functions:
   a. writePresence(userId) — calls navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition, then upserts { user_id, lat, lng, updated_at: new Date() } into the presence table. Returns { lat, lng } or throws.
   b. deletePresence(userId) — deletes the row from presence where user_id matches. Returns void.
2. A hook: src/hooks/useLocation.js that:
   - Returns { location, locationError, requestLocation }
   - requestLocation() calls writePresence and stores { lat, lng } in state
   - On unmount (or when called with cleanup=true), calls deletePresence
3. In the quest start flow (we'll build the screen later), show:
   - If location not yet granted: "Allow location to find nearby friends" button that calls requestLocation()
   - If location granted: show a green "◉ Location active" pill
   - If location denied: "Continue without location" option (solo quest)
Include error handling for: permission denied, timeout, position unavailable.
prompts.14:
Build the proximity matching system for Side/Quest.
Context:
- The "presence" table has: user_id, lat, lng, updated_at
- The "friendships" table has: user_id, friend_id, status, tier
- We need to find all users within 0.3 miles of the current user, ranked by distance
- Include direct friends AND friends-of-friends (2 hops)
Build:
1. A Supabase SQL function (write to supabase/proximity-function.sql) called get_nearby_users(my_user_id uuid, my_lat float, my_lng float):
   - Uses the Haversine formula to calculate distance in miles
   - Returns rows: user_id, name, avatar_color, distance_miles, tier ('friend', 'fof', 'open')
   - tier logic: if direct friend → 'friend'; elif friend of friend → 'fof' (include via_name); else → 'open'
   - Filters to distance_miles < 0.35
   - Orders by distance_miles ASC
   - Only includes presence rows where updated_at > now() - interval '30 minutes' (fresh location only)
2. src/lib/proximity.js — exported async function getNearbyUsers(userId, lat, lng):
   - Calls supabase.rpc('get_nearby_users', { my_user_id: userId, my_lat: lat, my_lng: lng })
   - Returns array of { userId, name, avatarColor, distanceMiles, distanceFt, tier, viaName }
   - distanceFt = distanceMiles * 5280, rounded to nearest 10
3. Output the full CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SQL
Note: at 50–100 users this full-table scan is fine. Add a comment noting that PostGIS would be needed at 10K+ users.
prompts.15:
Build the full Home screen for Side/Quest (src/screens/Home.jsx).
Design: dark ink (#1a1612) background, cream (#f4ede0) text, rust (#c44829) accents. Use Fraunces serif font (italic, bold) for display text, JetBrains Mono for labels, Bricolage Grotesque for body. Add these fonts to index.html Google Fonts link.
The screen has these sections from top to bottom:
1. Status bar row: "side/quest" logo left, user avatar initial (circle) right
2. Location pill: small pill showing "◉ [neighbourhood name]" if location known, "No location" if not. Tap to request location.
3. Quest state area (center, takes most of the screen):
   - If no active quest: large "◐" icon, italic "No quest active right now.", subtext "Next drop: today"
   - If active quest exists: "◉" icon in rust, italic "Quest in progress.", button "Resume quest →"
4. Streak badge: "🔥 Streak: X days" with a "FREEZE READY" pill if freeze available
5. Simulate drop button (for testing): small button at bottom of state area
6. Journal preview strip at the very bottom: label "Recent memories →", horizontal scroll of small square photo thumbnails (80x80px). If empty, show "No quests yet" placeholder.
Props/hooks to use:
- useAuth() for user data
- useQuestDrop() for active quest (from Task 10)
- useLocation() for location state (from Task 13)
- State from localStorage: streak count, lastQuestDate, freezeUsed
Wire up the "Resume quest →" and "Simulate drop" buttons to navigate to /quest and call the Edge Function respectively.
prompts.16:
Build the Quest Drop screen for Side/Quest (src/screens/QuestDrop.jsx), shown when a quest first lands.
Route: /quest-drop
It receives the active quest data via React Router state or by reading from useQuestDrop().
Design: full dark (#1a1612) screen with a tilted quest card in the center.
Layout top to bottom:
1. "▲ QUEST INCOMING ▲" in gold (#d4a02a), pulsing animation, small monospace caps
2. Current time displayed large (e.g. "4:17") in a big Fraunces serif font
3. Current date in small monospace below the time
4. Quest card — white/cream card, slightly rotated (-1.5deg), drop shadow:
   - "TODAY'S QUEST" red label tag in top-left corner (absolute positioned)
   - "VERIFY WITHIN 45 MIN" circle stamp in top-right
   - Context tag if available (e.g. "▾ TAILORED FOR YOUR AREA") in small rust text
   - Quest title in large italic Fraunces serif
   - Three meta pills at the bottom: DURATION, PARTY, RADIUS
5. "SEE WHO'S NEARBY →" CTA button (rust, full width)
6. "Skip today's quest" text link below
Clicking "SEE WHO'S NEARBY →" navigates to /nearby.
Clicking "Skip" sets a flag in localStorage and navigates back to /home.
prompts.17:
Build the Nearby screen for Side/Quest (src/screens/Nearby.jsx).
Route: /nearby
This screen shows nearby friends as pins on a stylised map, and lets the user select who to quest with.
The screen uses:
- getNearbyUsers() from src/lib/proximity.js (Task 14)
- writePresence() from src/lib/location.js (Task 13) — call this on mount if location available
Layout:
1. Back arrow (←) top left, "X within 0.3 mi" top right in italic serif
2. Map canvas — a 280px tall div with a grid background, showing:
   - A "you are here" red dot in the center with a pulsing ring
   - A dashed circle radius ring
   - Friend pins placed at random positions within the ring on mount (simulate positions)
   - Pin colors: green (friend), gold (friend-of-friend), blue (open/stranger)
   - Pins pulse with a CSS ring animation
   - Tapping a pin selects that person (same as tapping their list card)
3. Legend row: green = Friends, gold = Friends of, blue = Open
4. "▾ TAP TO BUILD YOUR PARTY" label
5. Scrollable list of nearby people as cards:
   - Avatar circle (colored by tier), name, tier label + meta, distance
   - Tapping selects/deselects (shows a ✓ and rust border)
6. "Or go solo →" text link
7. Floating bottom bar (appears when ≥1 selected): "X people selected — START QUEST →"

If no one is nearby: show "No friends nearby right now" with a "Go solo" button.
prompts.18:
Build the Active Quest screen layout and timer for Side/Quest (src/screens/ActiveQuest.jsx).
Route: /active-quest
This screen is shown while the user is completing a quest.
The screen has a dark (#1a1612) background.
Layout top to bottom:
1. Back arrow (←) — tapping shows a confirm dialog "Abandon quest? Your streak resets." If confirmed, deletes any in-progress session and goes to /home
2. Timer pill — shows "MM:SS LEFT" with a pulsing dot. Colors:
   - Default: rust (#c44829)
   - Under 15 min: orange (#e8702a)
   - Under 5 min: red with shake animation
3. "▲ QUEST IN PROGRESS" label (gold, monospace, small caps)
4. Quest title (large italic serif)
5. Quest description (small, 70% opacity)
6. Party row — avatars stacked with overlap, "PARTY OF X" or "GOING SOLO" label
7. Camera frame — a rounded rectangle (flex: 1, minimum 280px height). For now, shows a placeholder with "Tap to enable camera" and an enable button. We'll add real camera in the next task.
8. Shutter row at the bottom: FLIP button left, shutter circle center, SKIP button right
Timer logic:
- On mount, read expires_at from the active_quest (passed via router state or re-fetched)
- Calculate remaining = expires_at - Date.now()
- Update every second using setInterval
- When remaining <= 0: show a timeout modal and navigate back to /home
- Persist the quest start time to localStorage so refreshing doesn't lose it
Also: create a quest_session row in Supabase on mount with { quest_id, user_id, party_ids, started_at }. Store the session id in localStorage.
prompts.19:
Add real camera capture to the Active Quest screen (src/screens/ActiveQuest.jsx).
Build the camera system inside the camera frame area:
1. Camera enable flow:
   - "Allow camera access" button calls navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: { facingMode: 'environment' }, audio: false })
   - On success: hide placeholder, show <video> element with the stream
   - On error: show "Camera unavailable — tap SKIP to continue without a photo"
2. FLIP button:
   - Toggles facingMode between 'user' and 'environment'
   - Stops current stream, requests new stream with new facingMode
   - When facingMode is 'user': mirror the video with CSS transform: scaleX(-1)
3. Capture (shutter button):
   - Flash effect: a white overlay div briefly appears (opacity 1 for 80ms then 0)
   - Draws the video frame to a hidden <canvas> element
   - If front camera: mirror the canvas draw (ctx.translate + ctx.scale(-1,1))
   - Converts canvas to a blob (jpeg, quality 0.75)
   - Stores the blob in component state as capturedPhoto
   - Shows a preview: the captured image fills the camera frame area
   - After 1.2s, automatically calls completeQuest()
4. SKIP button: calls completeQuest() with capturedPhoto = null
5. completeQuest(photo):
   - Navigates to /memory with the photo blob and session data passed as router state
   - Does NOT yet upload to Supabase (that's the next task)
Handle: stream cleanup on unmount (stop all tracks).
prompts.20:
Upgrade the Active Quest camera to BeReal-style dual capture (src/screens/ActiveQuest.jsx).
Current state: single camera capture working (Task 19).
Add a "DUAL" toggle button in the top-left of the camera frame:
- Default state: DUAL OFF — single camera (back by default)
- When toggled ON:
  - Open a second stream with the opposite facingMode
  - Show a small PiP (picture-in-picture) window in the top-left corner of the camera frame: 100px wide × 140px tall, rounded corners, white border, showing the front camera
  - Main view: back camera
  - PiP view: front camera (mirrored)
Capture when DUAL is ON:
- Capture the main camera to mainCanvas
- Capture the PiP camera to pipCanvas (smaller: 300×420px)
- Store both as blobs: { mainPhoto, pipPhoto }
Pass both blobs to the memory card screen via router state.
Handle:
- If the device only has one camera (getUserMedia fails for second stream): show toast "Dual camera not available on this device" and stay in single mode
- Stop both streams on unmount
- When DUAL toggled off: stop the second stream, hide the PiP
Note: on iOS Safari, simultaneous dual streams are not supported. Detect this gracefully and show a message.
prompts.21:
Add photo upload to Supabase Storage and save the URL to the quest session.
Context:
- Supabase Storage bucket "quest-photos" exists (Task 12)
- The quest session row was created on mount in Task 18 with an id stored in localStorage
- After capture, we have a photo blob (and optionally a pip blob) in router state
Build src/lib/photos.js with:
1. async function uploadPhoto(blob, userId, sessionId, type = 'main'):
   - File path: {userId}/{sessionId}-{type}.jpg
   - Calls supabase.storage.from('quest-photos').upload(path, blob, { contentType: 'image/jpeg', upsert: true })
   - Returns the public URL via supabase.storage.from('quest-photos').getPublicUrl(path)
   - Handles errors — returns null on failure (don't block the flow)
2. In the memory card screen (which we build in Task 22):
   - On mount, call uploadPhoto() for mainPhoto and pipPhoto (if exists)
   - Update the quest_sessions row: { photo_url: mainUrl, pip_photo_url: pipUrl, completed_at: now(), elapsed_sec: elapsed }
   - Show a loading state while uploading ("Saving your memory…")
   - Show the photo once the URL is available (or use the local blob URL as a placeholder while uploading)
3. Also add pip_photo_url text column to quest_sessions:
   - Output the SQL: ALTER TABLE quest_sessions ADD COLUMN pip_photo_url text;
Include retry logic: if upload fails, try once more after 2 seconds before giving up silently.

prompts.23:
Build the Quest Journal screen for Side/Quest (src/screens/Journal.jsx).
Route: /journal
Accessible from the tab bar at the bottom of the home screen.
This screen shows all past completed quests.
Data: query quest_sessions where user_id = current user AND completed_at IS NOT NULL, ordered by completed_at DESC. Join with quests table for title.
Layout (cream #f4ede0 background):
1. Back arrow + "Journal" title (large italic serif)
2. Stats row (3 tiles):
   - Total completed (count of sessions)
   - Current streak (days)
   - Co-questers (count of unique user IDs across all party_ids arrays)
3. "▾ Recent quests" label
4. List of journal entries:

   - Tapping an entry expands it (expand in place)

Loading state: skeleton cards while fetching.
Empty state: "Your journal is empty. Complete a quest to start your archive." with a "▲ Drop a quest" button.
prompts.24:
Build the streak system for Side/Quest.
Rules:
- Streak increments by 1 each day the user completes at least one quest
- If a day passes without completing a quest, streak resets to 0
- One streak freeze is available per week — prevents reset on one missed day
- Streak and freeze data stored in Supabase (users table, add columns) so it persists across devices
Add columns to users table (output as ALTER TABLE SQL in supabase/streak-columns.sql):
- streak int DEFAULT 0
- last_quest_date date
- last_freeze_used_at timestamptz
Build src/lib/streak.js with these exported functions:
1. async function updateStreak(userId):
   - Fetches user row (streak, last_quest_date)
   - If last_quest_date = today: no-op (already counted today)
   - If last_quest_date = yesterday: streak + 1, update last_quest_date = today
   - If last_quest_date < yesterday or null: streak = 1, update last_quest_date = today
   - Updates user row in Supabase
   - Returns new streak value
2. async function checkAndResetStreak(userId):
   - Call on app open / home screen mount
   - If last_quest_date < yesterday AND streak > 0: check if freeze available (last_freeze_used_at < 7 days ago)
   - If freeze available: returns { streakLost: false, freezeAvailable: true } — show the freeze offer
   - If no freeze: resets streak to 0, returns { streakLost: true }
3. async function useFreeze(userId):
   - Sets last_freeze_used_at = now() in Supabase
   - Returns updated user
Call updateStreak() at the end of completeQuest() (in Task 22 memory card screen).
Call checkAndResetStreak() on Home screen mount.
prompts.25:
Build the quest timeout and streak reset modal for Side/Quest.
Triggers:
1. Timer reaches 0 in the Active Quest screen (Task 18)
2. User opens the app after a full day of missing a quest (from checkAndResetStreak in Task 24)
Build src/components/TimeoutModal.jsx:
- Overlay (semi-transparent dark background)
- Modal card (cream background):
  - Icon: a clock or "◴" character in rust, large
  - Title: "Time's up." or "You missed yesterday." (depending on trigger)
  - Body text: "Your quest window expired." / if freeze available: "Use your weekly streak freeze to keep your streak going?"
  - Primary button: "Back to home" → navigate to /home, reset current quest state
  - Secondary button (only if freeze available): "Use streak freeze 🧊" → calls useFreeze(), shows toast "Streak saved!", closes modal
Also build a global AppState context (src/context/AppState.jsx) that:
- Tracks: currentQuestSession, streak, freezeAvailable
- Provides: setCurrentQuestSession, resetQuest
- Wraps the whole app in App.jsx (update main.jsx)
- Used by Home, ActiveQuest, Memory screens to share quest state without prop drilling
Wire the timeout modal into ActiveQuest: when timer hits 0, show the modal instead of auto-navigating away.
prompts.26:
Add a persistent bottom tab bar to the Side/Quest app.

Build src/components/TabBar.jsx:

  1. Home — icon "◯" (or a simple SVG circle), label "HOME"

- Inactive tabs at 50% opacity white

- Uses useLocation() from react-router-dom to determine active tab
Show the TabBar:
- On /home route: visible
- On /journal route: visible
- On all other routes (/quest-drop, /nearby, /active-quest, /memory, /onboarding): hidden
In main.jsx: render TabBar at the root level, conditionally visible based on the current route.
Also update the Home screen to add padding-bottom: 80px so content doesn't hide behind the tab bar.
prompts.27:
Generate a CONTEXT.md file for the Side/Quest project that can be pasted at the top of every new Claude session to give instant context.
The file should include:
1. Project summary: what Side/Quest is in 3 sentences
2. Tech stack: React + Vite, Supabase, Tailwind, Vercel, React Router
3. File structure: list the key files and what each does (src/screens/, src/hooks/, src/lib/, src/components/, src/context/)
4. Supabase schema: all tables and their key columns
5. Design tokens: color hex values, fonts used
6. Current build status: a checklist of Tasks 1–26 with [x] for done and [ ] for not done yet (mark 1-26 all as done)
7. Known issues: a blank section to fill in
8. What to tell Claude: a note reminding the developer to always include this file + describe the specific task
Format it as clean markdown. It should be under 400 lines so it doesn't eat too much context window.
Write output to: CONTEXT.md in the project root.

Add a third tab "FRIENDS" to the TabBar (Task 26) with a person icon.

Join with users table to get name and avatar_color for each friend.
Layout (cream background):

2. Pending requests section (if any):
   - Cards showing incoming friend requests with "Accept" / "Decline" buttons

   - Decline: delete the friendship row
3. Friends list — each card:
   - Avatar circle (colored), name, "X quests together" (count from quest_sessions party_ids)
   - Small tier badge if they're a recent connection
4. Empty state: "No friends yet. Complete a quest near someone to connect."
5. A search bar at the top to search by name (client-side filter on the fetched list)
Also add a "+ Add friends" icon button in the top right that navigates to /add-friends (stub the route, we'll build it in Task 32).
prompts.29:
Improve the Nearby screen (Task 17) to use real Supabase presence data.
Current state: Nearby screen places friends at simulated random positions on the map.
Update the Nearby screen (src/screens/Nearby.jsx) to:
1. On mount: call writePresence(userId) to write current user's location to the presence table
2. Call getNearbyUsers(userId, lat, lng) from src/lib/proximity.js (Task 14) to get real nearby users
3. Display real nearby users in the list (instead of any hardcoded array)

6. Show a "last updated X seconds ago" label

prompts.30:
Build an in-app party invite system for Side/Quest.
Context: during a quest, a user can invite a specific friend to join their party.
Database: add a table (write to supabase/party-invites-table.sql):
CREATE TABLE party_invites (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  from_user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id),
  to_user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id),
  session_id uuid REFERENCES quest_sessions(id),
  status text DEFAULT 'pending',
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(),
  expires_at timestamptz DEFAULT now() + interval '5 minutes'
);
Build:
1. On the Nearby screen: each friend card gets a "Invite" button (small, secondary style)
   - Tapping inserts a row into party_invites
   - Shows "Invited ✓" and disables the button
2. A usePartyInvites hook (src/hooks/usePartyInvites.js):
   - Subscribes to INSERT events on party_invites where to_user_id = current user
   - When a new invite arrives: shows a toast banner at the top of the screen
   - Toast: "[Name] invited you to quest together — Join?" with Accept / Dismiss buttons
   - Accept: navigates to /active-quest with the session_id

prompts.39:
Add basic offline support to Side/Quest.
Context: the app is a PWA (Task 7). When users open it offline (e.g., during a quest in a park with bad signal), it should still work.
Build:
1. src/lib/cache.js — a simple localStorage cache layer:
   - set(key, data, ttlMs): stores { data, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs } under key
   - get(key): returns data if not expired, null otherwise
   - clear(key): removes the key
2. Wrap these Supabase calls with cache-then-network:
   - Active quest: cache for 5 minutes (key: 'active_quest')
   - Journal entries: cache for 10 minutes (key: 'journal_{userId}')
   - User profile: cache for 30 minutes (key: 'user_{userId}')
   - Nearby users: do NOT cache (location data is too stale)
3. Pattern to use in hooks:
   - On mount: show cached data immediately if available (no loading state)
   - Then fetch from Supabase in background
   - On success: update UI and refresh cache
   - On network error: keep showing cached data + show "Offline — showing cached data" label
4. Quest timer: the expires_at value is cached so the timer keeps counting down even offline.
5. Photo capture works offline: the blob is held in memory. Upload is attempted and if it fails, show a toast "You're offline — photo will save when you reconnect".
6. Service worker pre-caches the app shell (handled by vite-plugin-pwa from Task 7). No additional service worker changes needed.

prompts.42:
Add location context to the quest selection system.
When a quest drops, use the user's current location to pick a contextually appropriate quest.
Build src/lib/locationContext.js:
1. async function getLocationContext(lat, lng):
   - Calls the OpenStreetMap Nominatim reverse geocoding API (free, no key needed):
     https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse?format=json&lat={lat}&lon={lng}&zoom=16&addressdetails=1
   - Parses the response to determine context tags:
     - If display_name includes beach, coast, harbour, bay, pier, marina → add 'water'
     - If display_name includes park, garden, forest, reserve, common → add 'park', 'nature'
     - If address has city or town → add 'urban', 'commercial'
     - If address has village, suburb, neighbourhood → add 'suburban'
     - Always include 'universal'
   - Extracts a short human-readable label (neighbourhood, suburb, or city name)
   - Returns { contexts: string[], label: string }
   - Cache result in localStorage for 1 hour
2. Update the drop-quest Edge Function (supabase/functions/drop-quest/index.ts):
   - Accept an optional body param: { context_tags: string[] }
   - Filter quests WHERE context_tags && input_context_tags (array overlap in Postgres) OR 'universal' = ANY(context_tags)
   - If no context provided: pick any quest
3. In the Home screen "Simulate drop" button:
   - First call getLocationContext(lat, lng)
   - Pass context_tags to the drop-quest Edge Function
   - Update the location pill to show the detected label
4. In the quest drop screen: show "▾ TAILORED FOR [LABEL]" above the quest title if context was used.
prompts.43: Audit and optimise the Side/Quest PWA for performance. Run through these improvements: 1. Image optimisation: - In the journal and friends screens, all photo thumbnails should use lazy loading: - Photo URLs from Supabase Storage: append ?width=200&quality=60 to thumbnail URLs - Full-size photos (memory card): load at ?width=800&quality=80 2. Supabase query optimisation: - Add database indexes (write to supabase/indexes.sql): CREATE INDEX idx_quest_sessions_user_id ON quest_sessions(user_id); CREATE INDEX idx_quest_sessions_completed_at ON quest_sessions(completed_at DESC); CREATE INDEX idx_friendships_user_id ON friendships(user_id); CREATE INDEX idx_friendships_friend_id ON friendships(friend_id); CREATE INDEX idx_presence_updated_at ON presence(updated_at); - In the journal query: add .limit(20) and implement infinite scroll (load 20 more on scroll to bottom) 3. Bundle optimisation: - Lazy-load screen components with React.lazy() and Suspense in main.jsx - Ensure no heavy libraries are loaded globally 4. Lighthouse checklist: - Ensure all images have alt text - All interactive elements have aria-labels - Meta description and og:image tags in index.html Output all changed files.

prompts.46:
Design the feedback form to send to Side/Quest testers after their first quest.
Write the exact 5 questions, each with the question text, question type, and answer options.
The goal: learn whether the core loop is working before building more.
Design the questions to surface:
1. Whether the quest drop timing felt right (BeReal scarcity vs. annoying)
2. Whether they actually went outside and did the quest or gave up
3. Whether finding nearby friends worked (or felt awkward/pointless)

5. How likely they are to do it tomorrow (retention signal)
For each question include:

- Why this question matters (one sentence to help you interpret the answers)
Also include:

Format as a clear spec that can be entered directly into Google Forms.

prompts.47:
Add lightweight analytics to Side/Quest to track the 5 key metrics without any third-party analytics service.
The 5 metrics:
1. Drop-to-open rate: % of users who opened the app within 10 min of quest dropping
2. Open-to-start rate: % of openers who started the quest
3. Completion rate: % of starters who completed with a photo
4. Solo vs party rate: % who had a party vs went solo
5. Day 2 retention: did they complete a quest the next day
Build this using Supabase only:
1. A new table (write to supabase/analytics-table.sql):
CREATE TABLE analytics_events (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id),
  event text NOT NULL,
  properties jsonb DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
2. src/lib/analytics.js:
   - track(event, properties): inserts a row into analytics_events
   - Events to track:
     - 'quest_drop_seen': when the quest drop banner appears on home screen
     - 'quest_started': when user navigates to /active-quest
     - 'quest_completed': when memory card shows, include { had_photo, party_size, elapsed_sec }
     - 'quest_skipped': when user taps skip on the drop screen
     - 'quest_abandoned': when user taps ← on active quest and confirms
     - 'app_opened': on every app open (Home screen mount)
3. In the admin panel (Task 34): add a Metrics section that shows:
   - Completion rate: completed / started
   - Solo vs party: avg party_size from quest_completed events
   - All-time completed quests and DAU
Write SQL table to supabase/analytics-table.sql and the analytics.js lib file.
prompts.49:
Write a launch day runbook for the first real quest drop with 50 friends.
Format as a timeline with specific actions and what to watch for.
Include:
BEFORE THE DROP (day before):
- How to confirm everyone has installed the PWA (check admin panel user count)
- How to pick the right quest for a first drop
- How to pre-write the Signal/WhatsApp message to send when the quest drops
- What to do if fewer than 30 people have installed: send a follow-up nudge
LAUNCH MORNING:
- Set the cron to fire at a specific time (not random for the first drop — control it)
- Keep the Supabase dashboard open on: active_quest table, quest_sessions table
- What "success" looks like in the data within the first 10 minutes
DURING THE DROP WINDOW (45 minutes):
- When to post in the group chat (right when the cron fires)
- What metrics to watch live in the admin panel
- How to handle a bug in real-time
- What to do if the quest doesn't drop (manual fallback procedure)
AFTER THE DROP (same day):
- When to send the feedback form (2 hours after the drop)
- What the data should look like if the core loop is working
- Red flags: what numbers would make you worry
THE DAY AFTER:
- How to read the Day 2 retention metric
- How to follow up with people who didn't open
- What to iterate on first based on the feedback
Write output to: docs/launch-runbook.md
prompts.50:
Write a post-test decision framework for Side/Quest.
After running the 50-person test, the founder will have data. Help them decide what to build next.
Format as a decision tree: IF [metric/signal] THEN [what to build or do].
Cover these scenarios:
HIGH COMPLETION RATE (>60% completed their quest)
LOW COMPLETION RATE (<30%)
HIGH SOLO RATE (>70% went solo, didn't quest with anyone)
HIGH PARTY RATE (>50% quested with someone)
LOW DAY 2 RETENTION (<30% came back next day)
HIGH DAY 2 RETENTION (>50%)
QUALITATIVE SIGNALS to watch for in feedback:
- "I forgot it existed" → notification problem
- "I wanted to do it but no one was nearby" → proximity or friend graph problem
- "The quest felt lame" → quest design problem
- "I didn't understand what I was supposed to do" → onboarding problem
- "I loved it but my friends didn't" → cold start problem
End with: the one metric to obsess over in month 1 (retention) and when to know you're ready to scale.
Write output to: docs/whats-next.md
